allies.rationale_text: Cognitive-load-theory gives boundaries a concrete limit condition by showing why genuinely manageable chunks matter for real comprehension and execution.
allies.source_quote: the boundary of limited working memory

allies.rationale_text: Decomposition helps boundaries divide the work cleanly so scope becomes usable rather than abstract.
Prioritization sharpens boundaries by forcing choices about what deserves attention now and what belongs outside the active frame.
allies.source_quote: defining limits before engaging in complex tasks

antagonists.rationale_text: Theory-induced-blindness undermines boundaries when a familiar frame dictates the scope instead of the real objective and evidence landscape.
antagonists.source_quote: apply an existing model ... to a situation where it doesn't fit

structured_tensions.tension_text: Boundaries conflicts with systems-thinking when artificially narrow system boundaries hide feedback loops and interdependencies that cross the defined perimeter.
structured_tensions.source_quote: boundaries conflicts with systems-thinking (systems thinking) when artificially narrow system boundaries hide feedback loops and interdependencies that cross the defined perimeter.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Boundaries conflicts with optionality when prematurely fixed boundaries close off adjacent opportunities that emerge only through exploratory scope expansion.
structured_tensions.source_quote: boundaries conflicts with optionality (optionality) when prematurely fixed boundaries close off adjacent opportunities that emerge only through exploratory scope expansion.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strongest on cognitive, structural, and priority-setting allies plus one clean conflict with familiar-frame misuse, so the file keeps that compact surface.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor relation widening beyond the clearly named scoping and anti-frame-fixation neighborhood.
open_questions: Constraints are source-real partner pressure, but the retained set favors the more explicit current-vocabulary wording around decomposition, prioritization, and cognitive limits.
